sources.notes: Shared Div.2 entry; canonical problem is contest 2138C1.
Official topic confirms reducing the tree condition to depth-layer counts and subset-sum DP.
Luogu solution page used as a candidate source for cross-checking layer-count DP.

annotation.summary: Compute the best beauty value on a rooted tree in the easy version.
annotation.constraints: The easy version allows ordinary O(n^2) subset-sum/knapsack over depth counts.
annotation.core_idea: The tree condition reduces to deciding how many nodes can be selected from each depth layer. Count vertices by depth, then solve a subset-sum/knapsack over layer sizes to balance the two sides that determine the beauty.
tricks: Compress the tree into counts by depth.
Turn the tree beauty objective into subset-sum over layer sizes.

solution_variants.name: depth-count-knapsack
solution_variants.summary: DFS the tree to count nodes at each depth, then run ordinary knapsack/subset-sum over those counts.

tags.evidence: The main computation is subset-sum/knapsack over the depth-layer counts.
The tree is reduced to a multiset of depth-layer sizes before DP.
The original structure is a rooted tree and depth layers are obtained from it.
DFS/BFS is used to compute each node's depth.
The solution is a dynamic programming reduction over layer counts.
